ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon NTDS.dit extraction via 'ntdsutil ac i ntds ifm create' (CISA AA24-038A signature)
ucs.description: Hunts the exact LOTL credential-dump pattern fingerprinted in CISA AA24-038A and Microsoft's Volt Typhoon write-up: ntdsutil's Install-From-Media feature staging a full NTDS.dit copy to a temp folder. Volt Typhoon (Vanguard Panda / BRONZE SILHOUETTE) prefers ntdsutil over Mimikatz/secretsdump because it is signed, built-in, and avoids dropping non-Microsoft binaries on critical-infrastructure DCs.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="ntdsutil.exe" OR Processes.process="*ntdsutil*") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process "(?i)(?<ifm_target>create\s+full\s+\"?[a-z]:\\\\[^\"]+)" | where match(process, "(?i)ac\s+i\s+ntds") OR match(process, "(?i)ifm\s*\"?\s*create\s+full") | where NOT match(user, "(?i)\$$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process ifm_target
ucs.kql:
// Volt Typhoon — ntdsutil IFM dump of NTDS.dit (CISA AA24-038A)
// Adversary command from advisory:
//   cmd.exe /C "ntdsutil \"ac i ntds\" \"ifm\" \"create full c:\\windows\\temp\\Pro\" q q"
let _legit_dc_admin_hosts = dynamic([]); // populate with sanctioned DC-promo / restore jumpboxes
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                              // exclude machine accounts
| where DeviceName !in~ (_legit_dc_admin_hosts)
| where (FileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe" or ProcessCommandLine has "ntdsutil")
     or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "ntdsutil.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_all ("ifm", "create")          // IFM staging branch
     or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)ac\s+i(nstance)?\s+ntds"
     or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_all ("ifm", "create")
| extend StagingPath = extract(@"(?i)create\s+full\s+\"?([a-zA-Z]:\\[^\"\s]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession,
          StagingPath, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volt Typhoon — ntdsutil IFM dump of NTDS.dit (CISA AA24-038A)
// Requires GPO 'Include command line in process creation events' for 4688
let _legit_dc_admin_hosts = dynamic([]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where Computer !in~ (_legit_dc_admin_hosts)
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\ntdsutil.exe"
     or CommandLine has "ntdsutil"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\ntdsutil.exe"
| where CommandLine has_all ("ifm", "create")
     or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)ac\s+i(nstance)?\s+ntds"
| extend StagingPath = extract(@"(?i)create\s+full\s+\"?([a-zA-Z]:\\[^\"\s]+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, StagingPath
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon NTDS.dit Extraction via ntdsutil IFM
id: 5b9c6f32-7d3c-4d51-9f44-vt0001ntdsutil
status: stable
description: Detects Volt Typhoon's signature credential dump using ntdsutil 'ac i ntds' 'ifm' 'create full' to stage NTDS.dit on disk, per CISA AA24-038A and Microsoft Threat Intelligence reporting.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/05/24/volt-typhoon-targets-us-critical-infrastructure-with-living-off-the-land-techniques/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.003
  - attack.g1017
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    - Image|endswith: '\ntdsutil.exe'
    - ParentImage|endswith: '\ntdsutil.exe'
    - CommandLine|contains: 'ntdsutil'
  selection_ifm:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'ifm'
      - 'create'
  selection_acntds:
    CommandLine|re|i: 'ac\s+i(nstance)?\s+ntds'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and (selection_ifm or selection_acntds) and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Sanctioned DC promotion / forest recovery / IFM media generation by domain administrators
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic 'ntdsutil ran' fires on every legitimate DC-promo. Volt Typhoon's tradecraft is the IFM sub-command — they use it instead of vssadmin/secretsdump because it's signed, native, and produces no PE drop. The exact strings 'ac i ntds' and 'ifm create full <path>' are documented in CISA AA24-038A as the actor's verbatim invocation. Excluding machine accounts and a small allowlist of sanctioned D

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Volt Typhoon netsh portproxy v4tov4 redirector to KV-botnet SOHO infrastructure
ucs.description: Hunts Volt Typhoon (Voltzite / Vanguard Panda) establishing 'netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4' rules — their characteristic technique for turning compromised internal hosts into proxy hops that egress to KV-botnet-controlled SOHO routers (Cisco RV320/325, NETGEAR ProSAFE, ASUS), masking C2 traffic as benign LAN traffic. Documented across CISA AA24-038A, Lumen Black Lotus Labs, and Microsoft.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="netsh.exe" Processes.process="*portproxy*" by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process, "(?i)interface\s+portproxy\s+(add|set)\s+v4tov4") | rex field=process "(?i)listenport=(?<listenport>\d+).*?connectport=(?<connectport>\d+)" | rex field=process "(?i)connectaddress=(?<connectaddress>[0-9a-fA-F\.\:]+)" | where NOT match(user, "(?i)\$$") | where NOT cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8", connectaddress) AND NOT cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12", connectaddress) AND NOT cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16", connectaddress) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime host user parent_process_name process listenport connectport connectaddress
ucs.kql:
// Volt Typhoon — netsh portproxy v4tov4 to public IP (KV-botnet pivot)
// Adversary command per CISA AA24-038A:
//   netsh interface portproxy add v4tov4 listenport=8443 listenaddress=0.0.0.0 connectport=8443 connectaddress=<edge-router-ip>
let _it_admin_hosts = dynamic([]);  // populate with sanctioned admin jumpboxes
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where DeviceName !in~ (_it_admin_hosts)
| where FileName =~ "netsh.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_all ("interface", "portproxy")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("add", "set")
| where ProcessCommandLine has "v4tov4"
| extend ConnectAddress = extract(@"(?i)connectaddress\s*=\s*([0-9a-fA-F\.\:]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend ListenPort   = extract(@"(?i)listenport\s*=\s*(\d+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend ConnectPort  = extract(@"(?i)connectport\s*=\s*(\d+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend EgressesPublic = isnotempty(ConnectAddress)
     and not(ipv4_is_private(ConnectAddress))
     and not(ConnectAddress startswith "127.")
     and not(ConnectAddress startswith "169.254.")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession,
          ListenPort, ConnectPort, ConnectAddress, EgressesPublic
// Public-IP pivot is the high-fidelity Volt Typhoon signal;
// internal-only portproxy is left in for hunting tier triage.
| order by EgressesPublic desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volt Typhoon — netsh portproxy v4tov4 to public IP (KV-botnet pivot)
let _it_admin_hosts = dynamic([]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where Computer !in~ (_it_admin_hosts)
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\netsh.exe"
| where CommandLine has_all ("interface", "portproxy")
| where CommandLine has_any ("add", "set")
| where CommandLine has "v4tov4"
| extend ConnectAddress = extract(@"(?i)connectaddress\s*=\s*([0-9a-fA-F\.\:]+)", 1, CommandLine)
| extend ListenPort   = extract(@"(?i)listenport\s*=\s*(\d+)", 1, CommandLine)
| extend ConnectPort  = extract(@"(?i)connectport\s*=\s*(\d+)", 1, CommandLine)
| extend EgressesPublic = isnotempty(ConnectAddress)
     and not(ipv4_is_private(ConnectAddress))
     and not(ConnectAddress startswith "127.")
     and not(ConnectAddress startswith "169.254.")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, ListenPort, ConnectPort, ConnectAddress, EgressesPublic
| order by EgressesPublic desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volt Typhoon netsh portproxy v4tov4 Redirector
id: 7e2d9a1b-4f88-49bc-9c12-vt0002portproxy
status: stable
description: Detects creation of an IPv4-to-IPv4 portproxy via netsh — Volt Typhoon's signature method for chaining compromised hosts to KV-botnet SOHO router infrastructure for C2 obfuscation. Documented in CISA AA24-038A and Lumen Black Lotus Labs reporting.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa24-038a
  - https://blog.lumen.com/the-kv-botnet-from-the-prc-cyber-attack-perspective/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1090.001
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.g1017
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\netsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'interface'
      - 'portproxy'
      - 'v4tov4'
  selection_action:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'add'
      - 'set'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection and selection_action and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Network-administration tooling on documented jumpboxes
  - Hyper-V / WSL2 host-NAT helper scripts (allowlist by host)
level: high
ucs.rationale: netsh portproxy is rare in modern enterprises (port forwarders are normally a router function). Volt Typhoon's MO is to land on an internet-facing edge appliance, then plant a portproxy on internal pivot hosts that forwards lateral SMB/HTTP traffic out to a KV-botnet-controlled Cisco/NETGEAR SOHO router — making C2 look like routine egress from a corporate gateway. The combination of 'netsh interf
